files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives section at start after the 'Why learn this?' block. The What/Why/How framework is partially present but needs explicit structuring: move current 'Workflow' section into a 'What is CFD Workflow?' subsection with clear What/Why/How headings. Add Key Takeaways summary box at end before Concept Check. The solver selection tables are good - expand with 'When to use each' column for practical guidance.
files.01_External_Aerodynamics.md: Missing Learning Objectives entirely - add specific measurable outcomes like 'Calculate drag coefficient within 5% of experimental values'. The Overview section needs restructuring into explicit What (external aerodynamics simulation), Why (engineering applications), How (OpenFOAM implementation). Key Takeaways section missing - add summary of domain sizing rules, y+ guidelines, and force coefficient calculations. Concept Check questions are good but add practical troubleshooting scenarios.
files.02_Internal_Flow_and_Piping.md: No Learning Objectives - add outcomes covering flow regime identification, pressure drop calculation, and OpenFOAM BC setup. Overview table is useful but lacks context - add What/Why/How framework around it. The Darcy-Weisbach section needs 'Why this matters' explanation connecting to pump sizing. Missing Key Takeaways - summarize Re thresholds, entry length formulas, and common BC patterns. Add troubleshooting section for common convergence issues.
files.03_Heat_Exchangers.md: Missing Learning Objectives - add outcomes for CHT setup, effectiveness calculation, and validation methods. The '2 approaches' content is good What/Why but needs explicit How comparison table. Multi-region setup lacks step-by-step workflow - add numbered procedure. Missing Key Takeaways summary - consolidate NTU method, interface BCs, and validation criteria. Concept Check questions are excellent - keep this format. Add common pitfalls section for interface coupling failures.

global_strategy: Apply consistent 3W Framework across all files: (1) WHAT - Define the application domain with engineering context, (2) WHY - Explain physical importance and practical value, (3) HOW - Provide concrete OpenFOAM implementation steps. Each file needs: Learning Objectives at top (measurable outcomes), Key Takeaways before Concept Check (concise summary), and practical troubleshooting sections. Eliminate redundant content: solver selection tables appear in both 00_Overview and individual files - keep overview as high-level guide, move detailed configs to specific files. Function objects examples are duplicated - consolidate unique examples per application type. Add cross-references to turbulence modeling and heat transfer modules throughout.
